channel.title: 2026-06-11 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-11 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Efficient quantum information-inspired ansatz for variational quantum eigensolver algorithm: Applications to atomic systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Abdul Kalam, Prasenjit Deb, Akitada Sakurai, B. K. Sahoo, V. S. Prasannaa, and B. P. Das
Author(s): Abdul Kalam, Prasenjit Deb, Akitada Sakurai, B. K. Sahoo, V. S. Prasannaa, and B. P. Das We present a quantum information-inspired ansatz for the variational quantum eigensolver and demonstrate its efficacy in calculating ground-state energies of atomic systems. Instead of adopting a heuristic approach, we start with an approximate multiqubit target state and utilize two quantum informa… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023272] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Path superposition activating perfect quantum teleportation ability for separable states
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Sayan Mondal, Priya Ghosh, and Ujjwal Sen
Author(s): Sayan Mondal, Priya Ghosh, and Ujjwal Sen Quantum teleportation is a quintessential quantum communication protocol that enables the transmission of an arbitrary quantum state between two distant parties without physically transmitting the state with the help of shared entanglement and limited classical communication. We show that it is poss… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062431] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Device-independent secure correlations in sequential quantum scenarios
item.description:
📚 Quantum
👤 Matteo Padovan, Alessandro Rezzi, and Lorenzo Coccia
Quantum 10, 2131 (2026). https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2026-06-11-2131 Device-independent quantum information is attracting significant attention, particularly for its applications in information security. This interest arises because the security of device-independent protocols relies solely on the observed outcomes of spatially separated measurements and the validity of quantum physics. Sequential scenarios, i.e., where measurements occur in a precise temporal order, have been proved to enhance performance of device-independent protocols in some specific cases by enabling the reuse of the same quantum state. In this work, we propose a systematic approach to designing sequential quantum protocols for device-independent security. Our method begins with a bipartite self-testing qubit protocol…

item.title: Entanglement improving coordination in distributed systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Francisco Ferreira da Silva and Stephanie Wehner
Author(s): Francisco Ferreira da Silva and Stephanie Wehner Coordination in distributed systems is often hampered by communication latency, which degrades performance. Quantum entanglement offers fundamentally stronger correlations than classically achievable without communication. Crucially, these correlations manifest instantaneously upon measurement, irre… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062611] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Tensor network approach to momentum-resolved spectroscopy in nonperiodic super-moiré systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Anouar Moustaj, Yitao Sun, Tiago V. C. Antão, and Jose L. Lado
Author(s): Anouar Moustaj, Yitao Sun, Tiago V. C. Antão, and Jose L. Lado Computing spectral functions in large, nonperiodic super-moiré systems remains an open problem due to the exceptionally large system size that must be considered. Here, we establish a tensor network methodology that allows computing momentum-resolved spectral functions of noninteracting and interact… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023282] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Hybrid SU(1,1) Interferometry in Optomechanics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Chao Meng, Emil Zeuthen, and Polina R. Sharapova
Author(s): Chao Meng, Emil Zeuthen, and Polina R. Sharapova In nondegenerate SU(1,1) interferometers, beam splitters are replaced by two-mode squeezers, enabling sub-shot-noise sensitivity without input squeezing and robustness to detection losses by quantum entanglement. We propose a hybrid implementation in optomechanics where one “arm” is a mechanical mod… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 233602] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Liouvillian topology and nonreciprocal dynamics in open Floquet chains
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Florian Koch, Yu-Min Hu, and Jan Carl Budich
Author(s): Florian Koch, Yu-Min Hu, and Jan Carl Budich Open quantum systems far from thermal equilibrium can exhibit remarkable physical phenomena including topological properties without a direct equilibrium counterpart. Along these lines, in periodically driven-dissipative systems within the effective non-Hermitian (NH) Hamiltonian approximation spect… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023270] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Quantum finite-temperature Lanczos method
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Gian Gentinetta, Friederike Metz, William Kirby, and Giuseppe Carleo
Author(s): Gian Gentinetta, Friederike Metz, William Kirby, and Giuseppe Carleo The computation of thermal properties of quantum many-body systems is a central challenge in our understanding of quantum mechanics. We introduce the quantum finite-temperature Lanczos method (QFTLM), which extends the finite-temperature Lanczos method to quantum computers by combining real-time qua… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023285] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026

item.title: Dynamical distillation of topological boundary states via non-Hermitian masking
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Juan Kang, Chaohua Wu, Keran Li, Shuo Liu, Qinglong Zhang, and Gang Chen
Author(s): Juan Kang, Chaohua Wu, Keran Li, Shuo Liu, Qinglong Zhang, and Gang Chen Topological boundary states are a hallmark of topological phases, yet their direct detection remains challenging when they hybridize with bulk states or fail to form isolated in-gap modes. Here, we introduce a dynamical approach for directly distilling boundary-localized states in topological lattic… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063514] Published Thu Jun 11, 2026
